history: The client has a history of recurring conflicts and strained relationships, often marked by trust and boundary issues as well as aggressive behavior. Some relationships have involved alcoholism. The client frequently feels 'in the dark' about navigating relationships and seeks clarity and order through intellectual pursuits like mathematics. Immersion in mathematics serves as a protective barrier, providing a sense of control and predictability that is lacking in their interpersonal life. The client has recently chosen not to reconnect with an estranged person who previously caused harm, reflecting a cautious and self-protective approach.
unlovable_belief: I am bound to be abandoned
intermediate_belief: If I let people back into my life who have hurt me, I will get hurt again because I can't trust them to respect my boundaries. To stay safe, I need to keep my distance and rely on myself. It's better to avoid emotional risks than to risk being betrayed or harmed.
intermediate_belief_depression: Even if I try to set boundaries, it won't make a difference—people will still end up hurting me, so there's no point in trying to connect. I'm destined to be alone or misunderstood, and nothing I do will change that.
coping_strategies: The client immerses themselves in intellectual activities like mathematics to create a sense of control and clarity, distancing from the unpredictability of relationships. They avoid reconnecting with people who have caused harm as a self-protective measure. This avoidance and focus on structured pursuits serve as barriers against emotional distress but may also reinforce isolation.
situation: Considering reconnecting with an estranged person who previously caused harm. Deciding not to reconnect as a self-protective choice.
auto_thought: If I let them back into my life, I'll just get hurt again. I can't trust that things will be any different this time.
behavior: I decide not to reach out or respond to the estranged person. I focus on my own activities, like working on mathematics, to distract myself and feel safer.
